title: Human Liver Core-Needle Biopsy Processing Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3t24o","text":"Dissociating and isolating cells from a liver biopsy for single cell RNA-sequencing and storage for additional multiomic applications. We have developed a high-thoughput method of dissociating liver tissue for transcriptomic analysis, as well as preparing sections for histological, immunohistochemical and functional assessment. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"4s451","text":"All biological samples should be handled and processed according to UHN Biosafety Guidelines. This procedure should be performed in accordance with all applicable safety procedures and using aseptic technique.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Biopsy collection tube: Add 10 mL HBSS 1X to a 50 mL tube and keep it on ice or in the fridge for the liver biopsy collection (label the tube with the sample ID and date). All catalogue numbers in materials.
PBS-0.04% BSA: Add 100 µL of 2% BSA (same as 20 mg/mL) to 4.9 mL of PBS.
PBS-5% FBS: Add 2.5 mL of FBS to 47.5 mL of PBS.

Enzyme Master Mix: Add 50 µL collagenase + 50 µL protease to 0.4 mL of HBSS 1X. IMPORTANT: The enzyme mix must be prepared within 30 minutes of use and kept at 37ºC . 37 °C 1 x aliquot of collagenase (50 µL) (0.5m CDA units/mL, VitaCyte, 001-2030, Indianapolis, IN, USA) 1 x aliquot of protease (50 µL) (0.55 NP units/mL, VitaCyte, 003-1000, Indianapolis, IN, USA)

Transfer the biopsy to a petri dish and examine its size and whether it is possible to split it into 2 sections: one half for tissue dissociation and another half to then be split into two or three and be stored as 1) snap-frozen, 2) in OCT and/or 3) in formalin. Tip: Place the petri dish on a flat ice pack to section the biopsy while on ice
Transfer the biopsy section to be dissociated into a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube containing 200 µL of enzyme mix kept at 37ºC.
Using a sterile spring scissor cut the biopsy into as many pieces as possible. 1 x sterile spring scissor (14 mm cutting edge, 0.275 mm tip diameter, 12 cm length, Fine Science Tools)
Secure the Eppendorf tube inside a petri dish and place it in an incubator (37ºC) on a shaker set at 500 rpm for 10 min. 500 rpm, 37 °C, 10 minutes
Examine for the presence of undigested fragments. If there are undigested fragments, repeat Steps 8 and 9 to improve biopsy dissociation and digestion.

Filter the cell homogenate using a 70 µm cell strainer on a 50 mL conical tube or through the strainer of a 5 mL round-bottom FACS tube. Tip: Use a 20-200 µL pipette to transfer the cell suspension to central point of the cell strainer.
If the pellet is Pink/Red in color: Treat the cells with 1 mL of ACK lysing buffer for 5 minutes at room temperature. 00:05:00 Room temperature
Spin the cell suspension at 400g for 5min at 4ºC. 400 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes
Remove the supernatant.
Add 3ml of PBS-5% FBS.
Spin cell suspension at 400g for 5min 4ºC. 400 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes
Resuspend the pellet in 100ul of PBS-0.04% BSA.
Remove supernatant.
Transfer the cell suspension to a 1.5ml Eppendorf tube and keep the cells on ice for processing for 10x Genomics scRNA-seq. On ice
Wash the cell strainer with 3ml of PBS-5% FBS.
After completing the protocol, make sure to clean the scissors and send it for autoclaving (cover the sharp part with a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube and secure it with an autoclave tape).

Using the disposable scalpel and forceps, measure and the biopsy for dissociation and storage according to the below measurements: Length of core-needle liver biopsy is 2.4cm, measured with the rule at tail end of the scalpel. Lengths of core-needle liver biopsy allocated for different analyses and storage methods.
For tissue dissociation/scRNA-seq: Minimum biopsy length of ~1.0 cm should be allocated. Proceed to Step 7 to proceed with the dissociation of the biopsy.

For snap-freezing/OCT-embedding/formalin-fixing: Minimum biopsy length of ~0.5 cm for each storage method. Methods for snap-freezing, OCT-embedding and formalin-fixing are described in Human Liver Tissue Storage Methods For Multiomic Applications protocol.

Left over cells can be counted, spun down with the below specifications, and resuspended in 1ml of Freezing Media (10% DMSO in FBS) and placed in a cryovial for storage. 400 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes For viable freezing, transfer the cryovial to a Mr. Frosty container in a -80C freezer for 24 hours. Following this, transfer the cryovial to a Liquid Nitrogen Tank for long term storage.
creator: Diana Nakib
